lemma_statement.name: restricted_continuation_energy_identity_discharge_lemma
lemma_statement.claim: On the restricted-continuation region, the restricted energy identity is certified whenever the explicitly listed energy differentiability, restricted integration-by-parts, source-pairing, boundary-flux, and no-remainder hypotheses are supplied.

required_inputs.name: restricted_energy_functional_is_differentiable
required_inputs.role: supplies the time derivative of the restricted energy functional

required_inputs.name: state_time_regularitiy_for_energy_derivative
required_inputs.role: places the restricted-continuation state in the time-regularity class required to differentiate energy

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_integration_by_parts_identity
required_inputs.role: supplies the certified restricted integration-by-parts identity

required_inputs.name: restricted_source_pairing_exists
required_inputs.role: supplies the source/interior forcing pairing in the energy balance

required_inputs.name: restricted_boundary_flux_pairing_exists
required_inputs.role: supplies the certified boundary flux pairing term over partial Sigma_R

required_inputs.name: energy_identity_has_no_uncontrolled_remainder
required_inputs.role: ensures the restricted energy identity contains only certified interior, source, and boundary terms

discharged_obligations: Separates the energy identity from restricted integration by parts.
Separates the energy identity from boundary flux coercivity.
Certifies the exact assumptions under which the restricted-continuation energy identity is available.
Promotes the energy identity from an undischarged target obligation to a conditional discharge lemma.
non_claims: Does not prove the energy inequality.
Does not prove continuation or contradiction.
Does not prove full RR closure.
